evidence: Java camelCase naming conventions applied uniformly across all 43 source files — no snake_case drift
All HTTP handler classes follow XxxHandlers naming (ConcordanceHandlers, CorpusQueryHandlers, ExplorationHandlers, SketchHandlers, VisualizationHandlers) — consistent postfix
Internal request parameter records use XxxRequest naming (BcqlRequest, ConcordanceExamplesRequest, ValidatedExploreRequest, ValidatedSingleSeedRequest) — consistent
Options records follow XxxOptions (ExplorationOptions, SingleSeedExplorationOptions, FetchExamplesOptions) — consistent
parse*, build*, handle* verb prefixes applied consistently across handler classes

VisualizationHandlers.handleVisualRadial handles body-too-large by calling HttpApiUtils.sendError directly and returning, whereas CorpusQueryHandlers.parseBcqlRequest throws RequestEntityTooLargeException — inconsistent error-handling protocol across sibling handlers
SharingCategory overrides toString() returning label; RelationEdgeType overrides toString() returning label — consistent enum pattern
PosGroup.getValue() uses get-prefix; RelationEdgeType.label(), SharingCategory.label() do not — minor get-prefix inconsistency across sibling enums

strengths: Java naming conventions (camelCase, PascalCase for types) applied uniformly with no snake_case islands
Handler classes and their internal request/options records follow consistent naming patterns
Enum types consistently implement label() + toString() override for API-safe serialization
parse*, build*, handle* verb prefixes are codebase-wide conventions, not local patterns
convention_outlier.issue_character: Conventions are broadly consistent; the one clear outlier is VisualizationHandlers breaking the exception-based error signalling protocol that all other handlers follow for body-too-large conditions.
convention_outlier.score_rationale: The codebase is well-conventioned at the naming and structural levels. The score is held out of the mid-80s by the single handler that deviates from the established RequestEntityTooLargeException protocol, and by PosGroup.getValue() using a get-prefix while sibling enums RelationEdgeType and SharingCategory use the un-prefixed label() convention.

issues.summary: VisualizationHandlers.handleVisualRadial handles body-too-large by calling sendError directly instead of throwing RequestEntityTooLargeException like sibling handlers
evidence: CorpusQueryHandlers.parseBcqlRequest: throws new RequestEntityTooLargeException("Request body too large") — flows through wrapWithErrorHandling → 413
VisualizationHandlers.handleVisualRadial: calls HttpApiUtils.sendError(exchange, 413, "Request body too large"); return; — bypasses the exception-based contract
Both handlers are registered with wrapWithErrorHandling in WordSketchApiServer.registerRoutes()
issues.suggestion: In VisualizationHandlers.handleVisualRadial, replace the direct sendError call with throw new RequestEntityTooLargeException("Request body too large") to match the protocol established by CorpusQueryHandlers.

issues.summary: PosGroup.getValue() uses get-prefix accessor while sibling enums RelationEdgeType and SharingCategory use unprefixed label()
evidence: PosGroup: public String getValue() { return value; }
RelationEdgeType: public String label() { return label; }
SharingCategory: public String label() { return label; }
issues.suggestion: Rename PosGroup.getValue() to PosGroup.label() (or value()) to match the label() convention used by RelationEdgeType and SharingCategory. Update the single call site in GrammarConfigSerializer or wherever getValue() is used.

root_causes: PosGroup predates the label() convention; VisualizationHandlers body check was written before RequestEntityTooLargeException existed
